def test_extract_tar_dir_with_trailing_slash_mismatch():
    """
    Test that extraction fails when directory names in FILES.json have trailing
    slashes but the tar archive stores them without trailing slashes.

    This demonstrates the fragility of the _extract_tar_dir implementation which
    calls tar.getmember(dirname) directly without normalizing trailing slashes.
    """
    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:
        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, "test_collection.tar.gz")
        extract_dir = os.path.join(tmpdir, "extracted")
        os.makedirs(extract_dir)

        # Create a tar file without trailing slashes in directory names
        create_collection_tar_without_dir_trailing_slash(tar_path)

        # Try to extract using the buggy _extract_tar_dir function behavior
        with tarfile.open(tar_path, mode='r') as tar:
            # Simulate what happens in install_artifact:
            # FILES.json lists "plugins/" but tar has "plugins"
            dirname_with_slash = "test-collection-1.0.0/plugins/"
            dirname_without_slash = "test-collection-1.0.0/plugins"

            # This should fail with KeyError because tar has the name without slash
            # but we're looking for it with slash
            with pytest.raises(KeyError):
                tar.getmember(dirname_with_slash)

            # This succeeds because the tar has it without slash
            member = tar.getmember(dirname_without_slash)
            assert member.name == dirname_without_slash
            assert member.isdir()


def test_extract_tar_dir_with_trailing_slash_in_archive():
    """
    Test that extraction might fail when directory names in tar archive have
    trailing slashes but the code tries to look them up without slashes.

    This demonstrates the opposite scenario of the fragility.
    """
    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:
        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, "test_collection.tar.gz")
        extract_dir = os.path.join(tmpdir, "extracted")
        os.makedirs(extract_dir)

        # Create a tar file with trailing slashes in directory names
        create_collection_tar_with_dir_trailing_slash(tar_path)

        # Try to extract using tar.getmember with different forms
        with tarfile.open(tar_path, mode='r') as tar:
            # The tar has "test-collection-1.0.0/plugins/"
            dirname_with_slash = "test-collection-1.0.0/plugins/"
            dirname_without_slash = "test-collection-1.0.0/plugins"

            # This succeeds because the tar has it with slash
            member = tar.getmember(dirname_with_slash)
            assert member.name == dirname_with_slash
            assert member.isdir()

            # This will fail because tar has the name WITH slash
            # but we're looking for it without slash
            with pytest.raises(KeyError):
                tar.getmember(dirname_without_slash)


def test_tar_getmember_requires_exact_match():
    """
    Test demonstrating that tarfile.getmember() requires an exact match
    of the member name, including trailing slashes.

    This is the root cause of the fragility - directories can be stored
    with or without trailing slashes, and the lookup will fail if there's
    a mismatch.
    """
    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:
        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, "test.tar.gz")

        # Create a simple tar with a directory that has a trailing slash
        with tarfile.open(tar_path, mode='w:gz') as tar:
            dirinfo = tarfile.TarInfo(name="mydir/")
            dirinfo.type = tarfile.DIRTYPE
            dirinfo.mode = 0o755
            tar.addfile(tarinfo=dirinfo)

        # Verify the exact behavior
        with tarfile.open(tar_path, mode='r') as tar:
            # Exact match works
            member = tar.getmember("mydir/")
            assert member.name == "mydir/"

            # Without trailing slash fails
            with pytest.raises(KeyError):
                tar.getmember("mydir")

        # Now test the opposite: tar with no trailing slash
        tar_path2 = os.path.join(tmpdir, "test2.tar.gz")
        with tarfile.open(tar_path2, mode='w:gz') as tar:
            dirinfo = tarfile.TarInfo(name="mydir")
            dirinfo.type = tarfile.DIRTYPE
            dirinfo.mode = 0o755
            tar.addfile(tarinfo=dirinfo)

        with tarfile.open(tar_path2, mode='r') as tar:
            # Without trailing slash works
            member = tar.getmember("mydir")
            assert member.name == "mydir"

            # With trailing slash fails
            with pytest.raises(KeyError):
                tar.getmember("mydir/")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    pytest.main([__file__, "-v"])

    Test that extraction might fail when directory names in tar archive have
    trailing slashes but the code tries to look them up without slashes.

    This demonstrates the opposite scenario of the fragility.
    """
    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:
        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, "test_collection.tar.gz")
        extract_dir = os.path.join(tmpdir, "extracted")
        os.makedirs(extract_dir)

        # Create a tar file with trailing slashes in directory names
        create_collection_tar_with_dir_trailing_slash(tar_path)

        # Try to extract using tar.getmember with different forms
        with tarfile.open(tar_path, mode='r') as tar:
            # The tar has "test-collection-1.0.0/plugins/"
            dirname_with_slash = "test-collection-1.0.0/plugins/"
            dirname_without_slash = "test-collection-1.0.0/plugins"

            # This succeeds because the tar has it with slash
            member = tar.getmember(dirname_with_slash)
            assert member.name == dirname_with_slash
            assert member.isdir()

            # This will fail because tar has the name WITH slash
            # but we're looking for it without slash
            with pytest.raises(KeyError):
                tar.getmember(dirname_without_slash)


def test_tar_getmember_requires_exact_match():
    """
    Test demonstrating that tarfile.getmember() requires an exact match
    of the member name, including trailing slashes.

    This is the root cause of the fragility - directories can be stored
    with or without trailing slashes, and the lookup will fail if there's
    a mismatch.
    """
    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:
        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, "test.tar.gz")

        # Create a simple tar with a directory that has a trailing slash
        with tarfile.open(tar_path, mode='w:gz') as tar:
            dirinfo = tarfile.TarInfo(name="mydir/")
            dirinfo.type = tarfile.DIRTYPE
            dirinfo.mode = 0o755
            tar.addfile(tarinfo=dirinfo)

        # Verify the exact behavior
        with tarfile.open(tar_path, mode='r') as tar:
            # Exact match works
            member = tar.getmember("mydir/")
            assert member.name == "mydir/"

            # Without trailing slash fails
            with pytest.raises(KeyError):
                tar.getmember("mydir")

        # Now test the opposite: tar with no trailing slash
        tar_path2 = os.path.join(tmpdir, "test2.tar.gz")
        with tarfile.open(tar_path2, mode='w:gz') as tar:
            dirinfo = tarfile.TarInfo(name="mydir")
            dirinfo.type = tarfile.DIRTYPE
            dirinfo.mode = 0o755
            tar.addfile(tarinfo=dirinfo)

        with tarfile.open(tar_path2, mode='r') as tar:
            # Without trailing slash works
            member = tar.getmember("mydir")
            assert member.name == "mydir"

            # With trailing slash fails
            with pytest.raises(KeyError):
                tar.getmember("mydir/")

"""
Regression test for Galaxy collection tar directory extraction fragility.

This test demonstrates the issue where collection extraction fails when
tar archive directory entries have trailing path separators that don't
match what the code expects.

Issue: The _extract_tar_dir function in lib/ansible/galaxy/collection/__init__.py
calls tar.getmember(dirname) directly, which requires an exact match.
If the tar archive stores "plugins/" but the code looks for "plugins",
or vice versa, the extraction will fail with KeyError.

The root cause is that the code uses a fragile direct lookup instead of
handling the standard tarfile member naming conventions.
"""
import json
import os
import tarfile
import tempfile
from pathlib import Path
from unittest import mock

import pytest

def test_extract_tar_dir_with_trailing_slash_mismatch():
    """
    Test that extraction fails when directory names in FILES.json have trailing
    slashes but the tar archive stores them without trailing slashes.

    This demonstrates the fragility of the _extract_tar_dir implementation which
    calls tar.getmember(dirname) directly without normalizing trailing slashes.

    The issue: When FILES.json lists "plugins/" (with trailing slash)
    but tar has "plugins" (without), getmember() will raise KeyError.
    """
    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:
        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, "test_collection.tar.gz")

        # Create a tar file without trailing slashes in directory names
        create_collection_tar_without_dir_trailing_slash(tar_path)

        # Try to extract using tar.getmember() with mismatched names
        with tarfile.open(tar_path, mode='r') as tar:
            # The archive stores it as "test-collection-1.0.0/plugins" (no trailing slash)
            # But FILES.json might list it as "plugins/" (with trailing slash)
            # The collection root prefix from tar is "test-collection-1.0.0"
            dirname_with_slash = "test-collection-1.0.0/plugins/"
            dirname_without_slash = "test-collection-1.0.0/plugins"

            # This fails with KeyError because tar.getmember requires exact match
            # and the tar has the name without trailing slash
            with pytest.raises(KeyError):
                tar.getmember(dirname_with_slash)

            # This succeeds because the tar has it without slash
            member = tar.getmember(dirname_without_slash)
            assert member.name == dirname_without_slash
            assert member.isdir()

Regression test for Galaxy collection tar directory extraction fragility.

This test demonstrates the issue where collection extraction fails when
tar archive directory entries have trailing path separators that don't
match what the code expects.

Issue: The _extract_tar_dir function in lib/ansible/galaxy/collection/__init__.py
calls tar.getmember(dirname) directly, which requires an exact match.
If the tar archive stores "plugins/" but the code looks for "plugins",
or vice versa, the extraction will fail with KeyError.

The root cause is that the code uses a fragile direct lookup instead of
handling the standard tarfile member naming conventions.
"""
import json
import os
import tarfile
import tempfile
from pathlib import Path
from unittest import mock

import pytest


def create_collection_tar_with_dir_trailing_slash(tar_path):
    """Create a collection tar where directories have trailing slashes in their names."""
    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:
        # Create the collection structure
        coll_dir = Path(tmpdir) / "test_collection" / "ansible_collections" / "test" / "collection"
        coll_dir.mkdir(parents=True)

        # Create MANIFEST.json
        manifest = {
            "collection_info": {
                "namespace": "test",
                "name": "collection",
                "version": "1.0.0",
                "authors": ["Test Author"],
                "readme": "README.md",
                "tags": [],
                "description": "Test collection",
                "license": ["GPL-3.0-or-later"],
                "license_file": None,
                "dependencies": {},
                "repository": None,
                "documentation": None,
                "homepage": None,
                "issues": None,
            },
            "file_manifest_file": "FILES.json",
            "format": 1,
        }
        manifest_file = coll_dir / "MANIFEST.json"
        manifest_file.write_text(json.dumps(manifest))

        # Create FILES.json with directory entries that have trailing slashes
        files_manifest = {
            "files": [
                {
                    "name": ".",
                    "ftype": "dir",
                    "chksum_type": "sha256",
                    "chksum_sha256": None,
                },
                {
                    # Directory with trailing slash - this is the key issue
                    "name": "plugins/",
                    "ftype": "dir",
                    "chksum_type": "sha256",
                    "chksum_sha256": None,
                },
                {
                    "name": "plugins/modules/",
                    "ftype": "dir",
                    "chksum_type": "sha256",
                    "chksum_sha256": None,
                },
                {
                    "name": "plugins/modules/test_module.py",
                    "ftype": "file",
                    "chksum_type": "sha256",
                    "chksum_sha256": "e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855",
                },
            ],
            "format": 1,
        }
        files_file = coll_dir / "FILES.json"
        files_file.write_text(json.dumps(files_manifest))

        # Create the actual files and directories
        plugins_dir = coll_dir / "plugins"
        plugins_modules_dir = plugins_dir / "modules"
        plugins_modules_dir.mkdir(parents=True)

        module_file = plugins_modules_dir / "test_module.py"
        module_file.write_text("#!/usr/bin/env python\nprint('test')\n")

        # Create the tar file with directories that have trailing slashes
        with tarfile.open(tar_path, mode='w:gz') as tar:
            # Add the collection root
            arcname = "test-collection-1.0.0"

            # Add MANIFEST.json
            tar.add(
                str(manifest_file),
                arcname=f"{arcname}/MANIFEST.json",
                recursive=False,
            )

            # Add FILES.json
            tar.add(
                str(files_file),
                arcname=f"{arcname}/FILES.json",
                recursive=False,
            )

            # Add the plugins directory with trailing slash in the tar member name
            # This is the critical part - we store it with trailing slash
            plugins_tarinfo = tarfile.TarInfo(name=f"{arcname}/plugins/")
            plugins_tarinfo.type = tarfile.DIRTYPE
            plugins_tarinfo.mode = 0o755
            tar.addfile(tarinfo=plugins_tarinfo)

            # Add the plugins/modules directory with trailing slash
            modules_tarinfo = tarfile.TarInfo(name=f"{arcname}/plugins/modules/")
            modules_tarinfo.type = tarfile.DIRTYPE
            modules_tarinfo.mode = 0o755
            tar.addfile(tarinfo=modules_tarinfo)

            # Add the module file
            tar.add(
                str(module_file),
                arcname=f"{arcname}/plugins/modules/test_module.py",
                recursive=False,
            )

"""
Regression test for Galaxy collection tar directory extraction fragility.

This test demonstrates the issue where collection extraction fails when
tar archive directory entries have trailing path separators that don't
match what the code expects.

Issue: The _extract_tar_dir function in lib/ansible/galaxy/collection/__init__.py
calls tar.getmember(dirname) directly, which requires an exact match.
If the tar archive stores "plugins/" but the code looks for "plugins",
or vice versa, the extraction will fail with KeyError.

The root cause is that the code uses a fragile direct lookup instead of
handling the standard tarfile member naming conventions.
"""
import json
import os
import tarfile
import tempfile
from pathlib import Path
from unittest import mock

import pytest


def create_collection_tar_with_dir_trailing_slash(tar_path):
    """Create a collection tar where directories have trailing slashes in their names."""
    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:
        # Create the collection structure
        coll_dir = Path(tmpdir) / "test_collection" / "ansible_collections" / "test" / "collection"
        coll_dir.mkdir(parents=True)

        # Create MANIFEST.json
        manifest = {
            "collection_info": {
                "namespace": "test",
                "name": "collection",
                "version": "1.0.0",
                "authors": ["Test Author"],
                "readme": "README.md",
                "tags": [],
                "description": "Test collection",
                "license": ["GPL-3.0-or-later"],
                "license_file": None,
                "dependencies": {},
                "repository": None,
                "documentation": None,
                "homepage": None,
                "issues": None,
            },
            "file_manifest_file": "FILES.json",
            "format": 1,
        }
        manifest_file = coll_dir / "MANIFEST.json"
        manifest_file.write_text(json.dumps(manifest))

        # Create FILES.json with directory entries that have trailing slashes
        files_manifest = {
            "files": [
                {
                    "name": ".",
                    "ftype": "dir",
                    "chksum_type": "sha256",
                    "chksum_sha256": None,
                },
                {
                    # Directory with trailing slash - this is the key issue
                    "name": "plugins/",
                    "ftype": "dir",
                    "chksum_type": "sha256",
                    "chksum_sha256": None,
                },
                {
                    "name": "plugins/modules/",
                    "ftype": "dir",
                    "chksum_type": "sha256",
                    "chksum_sha256": None,
                },
                {
                    "name": "plugins/modules/test_module.py",
                    "ftype": "file",
                    "chksum_type": "sha256",
                    "chksum_sha256": "e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855",
                },
            ],
            "format": 1,
        }
        files_file = coll_dir / "FILES.json"
        files_file.write_text(json.dumps(files_manifest))

        # Create the actual files and directories
        plugins_dir = coll_dir / "plugins"
        plugins_modules_dir = plugins_dir / "modules"
        plugins_modules_dir.mkdir(parents=True)

        module_file = plugins_modules_dir / "test_module.py"
        module_file.write_text("#!/usr/bin/env python\nprint('test')\n")

        # Create the tar file with directories that have trailing slashes
        with tarfile.open(tar_path, mode='w:gz') as tar:
            # Add the collection root
            arcname = "test-collection-1.0.0"

            # Add MANIFEST.json
            tar.add(
                str(manifest_file),
                arcname=f"{arcname}/MANIFEST.json",
                recursive=False,
            )

            # Add FILES.json
            tar.add(
                str(files_file),
                arcname=f"{arcname}/FILES.json",
                recursive=False,
            )

            # Add the plugins directory with trailing slash in the tar member name
            # This is the critical part - we store it with trailing slash
            plugins_tarinfo = tarfile.TarInfo(name=f"{arcname}/plugins/")
            plugins_tarinfo.type = tarfile.DIRTYPE
            plugins_tarinfo.mode = 0o755
            tar.addfile(tarinfo=plugins_tarinfo)

            # Add the plugins/modules directory with trailing slash
            modules_tarinfo = tarfile.TarInfo(name=f"{arcname}/plugins/modules/")
            modules_tarinfo.type = tarfile.DIRTYPE
            modules_tarinfo.mode = 0o755
            tar.addfile(tarinfo=modules_tarinfo)

            # Add the module file
            tar.add(
                str(module_file),
                arcname=f"{arcname}/plugins/modules/test_module.py",
                recursive=False,
            )


def create_collection_tar_without_dir_trailing_slash(tar_path):
    """Create a collection tar where directories DON'T have trailing slashes."""
    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:

        # Create the actual files and directories
        plugins_dir = coll_dir / "plugins"
        plugins_modules_dir = plugins_dir / "modules"
        plugins_modules_dir.mkdir(parents=True)

        module_file = plugins_modules_dir / "test_module.py"
        module_file.write_text("#!/usr/bin/env python\nprint('test')\n")

        # Create the tar file WITHOUT trailing slashes in directory member names
        with tarfile.open(tar_path, mode='w:gz') as tar:
            arcname = "test-collection-1.0.0"

            tar.add(

            # Add the module file
            tar.add(

def test_extract_tar_dir_with_trailing_slash_mismatch():
    """
    Test that extraction fails when directory names in FILES.json have trailing
    slashes but the tar archive stores them without trailing slashes.

    This demonstrates the fragility of the _extract_tar_dir implementation which
    calls tar.getmember(dirname) directly without normalizing trailing slashes.
    """
    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:
        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, "test_collection.tar.gz")
        extract_dir = os.path.join(tmpdir, "extracted")
        os.makedirs(extract_dir)

        # Create a tar file without trailing slashes in directory names
        create_collection_tar_without_dir_trailing_slash(tar_path)

        # Try to extract using the buggy _extract_tar_dir function behavior
        with tarfile.open(tar_path, mode='r') as tar:
            # Simulate what happens in install_artifact:
            # FILES.json lists "plugins/" but tar has "plugins"
            dirname_with_slash = "test-collection-1.0.0/plugins/"
            dirname_without_slash = "test-collection-1.0.0/plugins"

            # This should fail with KeyError because tar has the name without slash
            # but we're looking for it with slash
            with pytest.raises(KeyError):
                tar.getmember(dirname_with_slash)

            # This succeeds because the tar has it without slash
            member = tar.getmember(dirname_without_slash)
            assert member.name == dirname_without_slash
            assert member.isdir()


def test_extract_tar_dir_with_trailing_slash_in_archive():
    """
    Test that extraction might fail when directory names in tar archive have
    trailing slashes but the code tries to look them up without slashes.

    This demonstrates the opposite scenario of the fragility.
    """
    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:
        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, "test_collection.tar.gz")
        extract_dir = os.path.join(tmpdir, "extracted")
        os.makedirs(extract_dir)

        # Create a tar file with trailing slashes in directory names
        create_collection_tar_with_dir_trailing_slash(tar_path)

        # Try to extract using tar.getmember with different forms
        with tarfile.open(tar_path, mode='r') as tar:
            # The tar has "test-collection-1.0.0/plugins/"
            dirname_with_slash = "test-collection-1.0.0/plugins/"
            dirname_without_slash = "test-collection-1.0.0/plugins"

            # This succeeds because the tar has it with slash
            member = tar.getmember(dirname_with_slash)
            assert member.name == dirname_with_slash
            assert member.isdir()

            # This will fail because tar has the name WITH slash
            # but we're looking for it without slash
            with pytest.raises(KeyError):
                tar.getmember(dirname_without_slash)


def test_tar_getmember_requires_exact_match():
    """
    Test demonstrating that tarfile.getmember() requires an exact match
    of the member name, including trailing slashes.

    This is the root cause of the fragility - directories can be stored
    with or without trailing slashes, and the lookup will fail if there's
    a mismatch.
    """
    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:
        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, "test.tar.gz")

        # Create a simple tar with a directory that has a trailing slash
        with tarfile.open(tar_path, mode='w:gz') as tar:
            dirinfo = tarfile.TarInfo(name="mydir/")
            dirinfo.type = tarfile.DIRTYPE
            dirinfo.mode = 0o755
            tar.addfile(tarinfo=dirinfo)

        # Verify the exact behavior
        with tarfile.open(tar_path, mode='r') as tar:
            # Exact match works
            member = tar.getmember("mydir/")
            assert member.name == "mydir/"

            # Without trailing slash fails
            with pytest.raises(KeyError):
                tar.getmember("mydir")

        # Now test the opposite: tar with no trailing slash
        tar_path2 = os.path.join(tmpdir, "test2.tar.gz")
        with tarfile.open(tar_path2, mode='w:gz') as tar:
            dirinfo = tarfile.TarInfo(name="mydir")
            dirinfo.type = tarfile.DIRTYPE
            dirinfo.mode = 0o755
            tar.addfile(tarinfo=dirinfo)

        with tarfile.open(tar_path2, mode='r') as tar:
            # Without trailing slash works
            member = tar.getmember("mydir")
            assert member.name == "mydir"

            # With trailing slash fails
            with pytest.raises(KeyError):
                tar.getmember("mydir/")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    pytest.main([__file__, "-v"])

                str(manifest_file),
                arcname=f"{arcname}/MANIFEST.json",
                recursive=False,
            )

            # Add FILES.json
            tar.add(
                str(files_file),
                arcname=f"{arcname}/FILES.json",
                recursive=False,
            )

            # Add the plugins directory WITHOUT trailing slash
            plugins_tarinfo = tarfile.TarInfo(name=f"{arcname}/plugins")
            plugins_tarinfo.type = tarfile.DIRTYPE
            plugins_tarinfo.mode = 0o755
            tar.addfile(tarinfo=plugins_tarinfo)

            # Add the plugins/modules directory WITHOUT trailing slash
            modules_tarinfo = tarfile.TarInfo(name=f"{arcname}/plugins/modules")
            modules_tarinfo.type = tarfile.DIRTYPE
            modules_tarinfo.mode = 0o755
            tar.addfile(tarinfo=modules_tarinfo)

            # Add the module file
            tar.add(
                str(module_file),
                arcname=f"{arcname}/plugins/modules/test_module.py",
                recursive=False,
            )


def test_extract_tar_dir_with_trailing_slash_mismatch():
    """
    Test that extraction fails when directory names in FILES.json have trailing
    slashes but the tar archive stores them without trailing slashes.

    This demonstrates the fragility of the _extract_tar_dir implementation which
    calls tar.getmember(dirname) directly without normalizing trailing slashes.
    """
    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:
        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, "test_collection.tar.gz")
        extract_dir = os.path.join(tmpdir, "extracted")
        os.makedirs(extract_dir)

        # Create a tar file without trailing slashes in directory names
        create_collection_tar_without_dir_trailing_slash(tar_path)

        # Try to extract using the buggy _extract_tar_dir function behavior
        with tarfile.open(tar_path, mode='r') as tar:
            # Simulate what happens in install_artifact:
            # FILES.json lists "plugins/" but tar has "plugins"
            dirname_with_slash = "test-collection-1.0.0/plugins/"
            dirname_without_slash = "test-collection-1.0.0/plugins"

            # This should fail with KeyError because tar has the name without slash
            # but we're looking for it with slash
            with pytest.raises(KeyError):
                tar.getmember(dirname_with_slash)

            # This succeeds because the tar has it without slash
            member = tar.getmember(dirname_without_slash)
            assert member.name == dirname_without_slash
            assert member.isdir()


def test_extract_tar_dir_with_trailing_slash_in_archive():
    """
    Test that extraction might fail when directory names in tar archive have
    trailing slashes but the code tries to look them up without slashes.

    This demonstrates the opposite scenario of the fragility.
    """
    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:
        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, "test_collection.tar.gz")
        extract_dir = os.path.join(tmpdir, "extracted")
        os.makedirs(extract_dir)

        # Create a tar file with trailing slashes in directory names
        create_collection_tar_with_dir_trailing_slash(tar_path)

        # Try to extract using tar.getmember with different forms
        with tarfile.open(tar_path, mode='r') as tar:
            # The tar has "test-collection-1.0.0/plugins/"
            dirname_with_slash = "test-collection-1.0.0/plugins/"
            dirname_without_slash = "test-collection-1.0.0/plugins"

            # This succeeds because the tar has it with slash
            member = tar.getmember(dirname_with_slash)
            assert member.name == dirname_with_slash
            assert member.isdir()

            # This will fail because tar has the name WITH slash
            # but we're looking for it without slash
            with pytest.raises(KeyError):
                tar.getmember(dirname_without_slash)


def test_tar_getmember_requires_exact_match():
    """
    Test demonstrating that tarfile.getmember() requires an exact match
    of the member name, including trailing slashes.

    This is the root cause of the fragility - directories can be stored
    with or without trailing slashes, and the lookup will fail if there's
    a mismatch.
    """
    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:
        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, "test.tar.gz")

        # Create a simple tar with a directory that has a trailing slash
        with tarfile.open(tar_path, mode='w:gz') as tar:
            dirinfo = tarfile.TarInfo(name="mydir/")
            dirinfo.type = tarfile.DIRTYPE
            dirinfo.mode = 0o755
            tar.addfile(tarinfo=dirinfo)

        # Verify the exact behavior
        with tarfile.open(tar_path, mode='r') as tar:
            # Exact match works
            member = tar.getmember("mydir/")
            assert member.name == "mydir/"

            # Without trailing slash fails
            with pytest.raises(KeyError):
                tar.getmember("mydir")

        # Now test the opposite: tar with no trailing slash
        tar_path2 = os.path.join(tmpdir, "test2.tar.gz")
        with tarfile.open(tar_path2, mode='w:gz') as tar:
            dirinfo = tarfile.TarInfo(name="mydir")
            dirinfo.type = tarfile.DIRTYPE
            dirinfo.mode = 0o755
            tar.addfile(tarinfo=dirinfo)

        with tarfile.open(tar_path2, mode='r') as tar:
            # Without trailing slash works
            member = tar.getmember("mydir")
            assert member.name == "mydir"

            # With trailing slash fails
            with pytest.raises(KeyError):
                tar.getmember("mydir/")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    pytest.main([__file__, "-v"])

"""
Regression test for Galaxy collection tar directory extraction fragility.

This test demonstrates the issue where collection extraction fails when
tar archive directory entries have trailing path separators that don't
match what the code expects.

Issue: The _extract_tar_dir function in lib/ansible/galaxy/collection/__init__.py
calls tar.getmember(dirname) directly, which requires an exact match.
If the tar archive stores "plugins/" but the code looks for "plugins",
or vice versa, the extraction will fail with KeyError.

The root cause is that the code uses a fragile direct lookup instead of
handling the standard tarfile member naming conventions.
"""
import json
import os
import tarfile
import tempfile
from pathlib import Path
from unittest import mock

import pytest


def create_collection_tar_with_dir_trailing_slash(tar_path):
    """Create a collection tar where directories have trailing slashes in their names."""
    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:
        # Create the collection structure
        coll_dir = Path(tmpdir) / "test_collection" / "ansible_collections" / "test" / "collection"
        coll_dir.mkdir(parents=True)

        # Create MANIFEST.json
        manifest = {
            "collection_info": {
                "namespace": "test",
                "name": "collection",
                "version": "1.0.0",

                "license": ["GPL-3.0-or-later"],

                "dependencies": {},
                "repository": None,
                "documentation": None,
                "homepage": None,
                "issues": None,

            "file_manifest_file": "FILES.json",
            "format": 1,

        manifest_file.write_text(json.dumps(manifest))

        files_manifest = {

                {

                arcname=f"{arcname}/plugins/modules/test_module.py",
                recursive=False,
            )

arguments.new_string:
"""
Regression test for Galaxy collection tar directory extraction fragility.

This test demonstrates the issue where collection extraction fails when
tar archive directory entries have trailing path separators that don't
match what the code expects.

Issue: The _extract_tar_dir function in lib/ansible/galaxy/collection/__init__.py
calls tar.getmember(dirname) directly without normalizing trailing slashes.
This creates a fragile situation where:

1. FILES.json might list directories with trailing slashes (e.g., "plugins/")
2. But the tar archive stores the directory name in a different format
3. When getmember() is called, the lookup fails with KeyError

The root cause is that the code uses a direct getmember() call without
any robust lookup strategy that accounts for naming variations.
"""
import json
import os
import tarfile
import tempfile
from pathlib import Path

import pytest

Regression test for Galaxy collection tar directory extraction fragility.

This test demonstrates the issue where collection extraction fails when
tar archive directory entries have trailing path separators that don't
match what the code expects.

Issue: The _extract_tar_dir function in lib/ansible/galaxy/collection/__init__.py
calls tar.getmember(dirname) directly without normalizing trailing slashes.
This creates a fragile situation where:

1. FILES.json might list directories with trailing slashes (e.g., "plugins/")
2. But the tar archive stores the directory name in a different format
3. When getmember() is called, the lookup fails with KeyError

The root cause is that the code uses a direct getmember() call without
any robust lookup strategy that accounts for naming variations.

"""
Regression test for Galaxy collection tar directory extraction fragility.

This test demonstrates the issue where collection extraction fails when
tar archive directory entries have trailing path separators that don't
match what the code expects.

Issue: The _extract_tar_dir function in lib/ansible/galaxy/collection/__init__.py
calls tar.getmember(dirname) directly, which requires an exact match.
If the tar archive stores "plugins/" but the code looks for "plugins",
or vice versa, the extraction will fail with KeyError.

The root cause is that the code uses a fragile direct lookup instead of
handling the standard tarfile member naming conventions.
"""
import json
import os
import tarfile
import tempfile
from pathlib import Path
from unittest import mock

import pytest


def create_collection_tar_with_dir_trailing_slash(tar_path):
    """Create a collection tar where directories have trailing slashes in their names."""
    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:
        # Create the collection structure
        coll_dir = Path(tmpdir) / "test_collection" / "ansible_collections" / "test" / "collection"
        coll_dir.mkdir(parents=True)

        # Create MANIFEST.json
        manifest = {
            "collection_info": {
                "namespace": "test",
                "name": "collection",
                "version": "1.0.0",
                "authors": ["Test Author"],
                "readme": "README.md",
                "tags": [],
                "description": "Test collection",
                "license": ["GPL-3.0-or-later"],
                "license_file": None,
                "dependencies": {},
                "repository": None,
                "documentation": None,
                "homepage": None,
                "issues": None,
            },
            "file_manifest_file": "FILES.json",
            "format": 1,
        }
        manifest_file = coll_dir / "MANIFEST.json"
        manifest_file.write_text(json.dumps(manifest))

        # Create FILES.json with directory entries that have trailing slashes
        files_manifest = {
            "files": [
                {
                    "name": ".",
                    "ftype": "dir",
                    "chksum_type": "sha256",
                    "chksum_sha256": None,
                },
                {
                    # Directory with trailing slash - this is the key issue
                    "name": "plugins/",
                    "ftype": "dir",
                    "chksum_type": "sha256",
                    "chksum_sha256": None,
                },
                {
                    "name": "plugins/modules/",
                    "ftype": "dir",
                    "chksum_type": "sha256",
                    "chksum_sha256": None,
                },
                {
                    "name": "plugins/modules/test_module.py",
                    "ftype": "file",
                    "chksum_type": "sha256",
                    "chksum_sha256": "e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855",
                },
            ],
            "format": 1,
        }
        files_file = coll_dir / "FILES.json"
        files_file.write_text(json.dumps(files_manifest))

        # Create the actual files and directories
        plugins_dir = coll_dir / "plugins"
        plugins_modules_dir = plugins_dir / "modules"
        plugins_modules_dir.mkdir(parents=True)

        module_file = plugins_modules_dir / "test_module.py"
        module_file.write_text("#!/usr/bin/env python\nprint('test')\n")

        # Create the tar file with directories that have trailing slashes
        with tarfile.open(tar_path, mode='w:gz') as tar:
            # Add the collection root
            arcname = "test-collection-1.0.0"

            # Add MANIFEST.json
            tar.add(
                str(manifest_file),
                arcname=f"{arcname}/MANIFEST.json",
                recursive=False,
            )

            # Add FILES.json
            tar.add(
                str(files_file),
                arcname=f"{arcname}/FILES.json",
                recursive=False,
            )

            # Add the plugins directory with trailing slash in the tar member name
            # This is the critical part - we store it with trailing slash
            plugins_tarinfo = tarfile.TarInfo(name=f"{arcname}/plugins/")
            plugins_tarinfo.type = tarfile.DIRTYPE
            plugins_tarinfo.mode = 0o755
            tar.addfile(tarinfo=plugins_tarinfo)

            # Add the plugins/modules directory with trailing slash
            modules_tarinfo = tarfile.TarInfo(name=f"{arcname}/plugins/modules/")
            modules_tarinfo.type = tarfile.DIRTYPE
            modules_tarinfo.mode = 0o755
            tar.addfile(tarinfo=modules_tarinfo)

            # Add the module file
            tar.add(
                str(module_file),
                arcname=f"{arcname}/plugins/modules/test_module.py",
                recursive=False,
            )

"""
Regression test for Galaxy collection tar directory extraction fragility.

This test demonstrates the issue where collection extraction fails when
tar archive directory entries have trailing path separators that don't
match what the code expects.

Issue: The _extract_tar_dir function in lib/ansible/galaxy/collection/__init__.py
calls tar.getmember(dirname) directly without normalizing trailing slashes.
This creates a fragile situation where:

1. FILES.json might list directories with trailing slashes (e.g., "plugins/")
2. But the tar archive stores the directory name in a different format
3. When getmember() is called, the lookup fails with KeyError

The root cause is that the code uses a direct getmember() call without
any robust lookup strategy that accounts for naming variations.
"""
import json
import os
import tarfile
import tempfile
from pathlib import Path

import pytest

def test_extract_tar_dir_with_trailing_slash_mismatch():
    """
    Test that extraction fails when directory names in FILES.json have trailing
    slashes but the tar archive stores them without trailing slashes.

    This demonstrates the fragility of the _extract_tar_dir implementation which
    calls tar.getmember(dirname) directly without normalizing trailing slashes.

    The issue: When FILES.json lists "plugins/" (with trailing slash)
    but tar has "plugins" (without), getmember() will raise KeyError.
    """
    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:
        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, "test_collection.tar.gz")

        # Create a tar file without trailing slashes in directory names
        create_collection_tar_without_dir_trailing_slash(tar_path)

        # Try to extract using tar.getmember() with mismatched names
        with tarfile.open(tar_path, mode='r') as tar:
            # The archive stores it as "test-collection-1.0.0/plugins" (no trailing slash)
            # But FILES.json might list it as "plugins/" (with trailing slash)
            # The collection root prefix from tar is "test-collection-1.0.0"
            dirname_with_slash = "test-collection-1.0.0/plugins/"
            dirname_without_slash = "test-collection-1.0.0/plugins"

            # This fails with KeyError because tar.getmember requires exact match
            # and the tar has the name without trailing slash
            with pytest.raises(KeyError):
                tar.getmember(dirname_with_slash)

            # This succeeds because the tar has it without slash
            member = tar.getmember(dirname_without_slash)
            assert member.name == dirname_without_slash
            assert member.isdir()


def test_extract_tar_dir_with_trailing_slash_in_archive():
    """
    Test that extraction might fail when directory names in tar archive have
    trailing slashes but the code tries to look them up without slashes.

    This demonstrates the opposite scenario of the fragility.
    """
    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:
        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, "test_collection.tar.gz")
        extract_dir = os.path.join(tmpdir, "extracted")
        os.makedirs(extract_dir)

        # Create a tar file with trailing slashes in directory names
        create_collection_tar_with_dir_trailing_slash(tar_path)

        # Try to extract using tar.getmember with different forms
        with tarfile.open(tar_path, mode='r') as tar:
            # The tar has "test-collection-1.0.0/plugins/"
            dirname_with_slash = "test-collection-1.0.0/plugins/"
            dirname_without_slash = "test-collection-1.0.0/plugins"

            # This succeeds because the tar has it with slash
            member = tar.getmember(dirname_with_slash)
            assert member.name == dirname_with_slash
            assert member.isdir()

            # This will fail because tar has the name WITH slash
            # but we're looking for it without slash
            with pytest.raises(KeyError):
                tar.getmember(dirname_without_slash)


def test_tar_getmember_requires_exact_match():
    """
    Test demonstrating that tarfile.getmember() requires an exact match
    of the member name, including trailing slashes.

    This is the root cause of the fragility - directories can be stored
    with or without trailing slashes, and the lookup will fail if there's
    a mismatch.
    """
    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:
        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, "test.tar.gz")

        # Create a simple tar with a directory that has a trailing slash
        with tarfile.open(tar_path, mode='w:gz') as tar:
            dirinfo = tarfile.TarInfo(name="mydir/")
            dirinfo.type = tarfile.DIRTYPE
            dirinfo.mode = 0o755
            tar.addfile(tarinfo=dirinfo)

        # Verify the exact behavior
        with tarfile.open(tar_path, mode='r') as tar:
            # Exact match works
            member = tar.getmember("mydir/")
            assert member.name == "mydir/"

            # Without trailing slash fails
            with pytest.raises(KeyError):
                tar.getmember("mydir")

        # Now test the opposite: tar with no trailing slash
        tar_path2 = os.path.join(tmpdir, "test2.tar.gz")
        with tarfile.open(tar_path2, mode='w:gz') as tar:
            dirinfo = tarfile.TarInfo(name="mydir")
            dirinfo.type = tarfile.DIRTYPE
            dirinfo.mode = 0o755
            tar.addfile(tarinfo=dirinfo)

        with tarfile.open(tar_path2, mode='r') as tar:
            # Without trailing slash works
            member = tar.getmember("mydir")
            assert member.name == "mydir"

            # With trailing slash fails
            with pytest.raises(KeyError):
                tar.getmember("mydir/")

def test_extract_tar_dir_with_trailing_slash_mismatch():
    """
    Test that extraction fails when directory names in FILES.json have trailing
    slashes but the tar archive stores them without trailing slashes.

    This demonstrates the fragility of the _extract_tar_dir implementation which
    calls tar.getmember(dirname) directly without normalizing trailing slashes.

    The issue: When FILES.json lists "plugins/" (with trailing slash)
    but tar has "plugins" (without), getmember() will raise KeyError.
    """
    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:
        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, "test_collection.tar.gz")

        # Create a tar file without trailing slashes in directory names
        create_collection_tar_without_dir_trailing_slash(tar_path)

        # Try to extract using tar.getmember() with mismatched names
        with tarfile.open(tar_path, mode='r') as tar:
            # The archive stores it as "test-collection-1.0.0/plugins" (no trailing slash)
            # But FILES.json might list it as "plugins/" (with trailing slash)
            # The collection root prefix from tar is "test-collection-1.0.0"
            dirname_with_slash = "test-collection-1.0.0/plugins/"
            dirname_without_slash = "test-collection-1.0.0/plugins"

            # This fails with KeyError because tar.getmember requires exact match
            # and the tar has the name without trailing slash
            with pytest.raises(KeyError):
                tar.getmember(dirname_with_slash)

            # This succeeds because the tar has it without slash
            member = tar.getmember(dirname_without_slash)
            assert member.name == dirname_without_slash
            assert member.isdir()


def test_extract_tar_dir_with_trailing_slash_in_archive():
    """
    Test that extraction might fail when directory names in tar archive have
    trailing slashes but the code tries to look them up without slashes.

    This demonstrates the opposite scenario of the fragility.
    """
    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:
        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, "test_collection.tar.gz")
        extract_dir = os.path.join(tmpdir, "extracted")
        os.makedirs(extract_dir)

        # Create a tar file with trailing slashes in directory names
        create_collection_tar_with_dir_trailing_slash(tar_path)

        # Try to extract using tar.getmember with different forms
        with tarfile.open(tar_path, mode='r') as tar:
            # The tar has "test-collection-1.0.0/plugins/"
            dirname_with_slash = "test-collection-1.0.0/plugins/"
            dirname_without_slash = "test-collection-1.0.0/plugins"

            # This succeeds because the tar has it with slash
            member = tar.getmember(dirname_with_slash)
            assert member.name == dirname_with_slash
            assert member.isdir()

            # This will fail because tar has the name WITH slash
            # but we're looking for it without slash
            with pytest.raises(KeyError):
                tar.getmember(dirname_without_slash)


def test_tar_getmember_requires_exact_match():
    """
    Test demonstrating that tarfile.getmember() requires an exact match
    of the member name, including trailing slashes.

    This is the root cause of the fragility - directories can be stored
    with or without trailing slashes, and the lookup will fail if there's
    a mismatch.
    """
    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:
        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, "test.tar.gz")

        # Create a simple tar with a directory that has a trailing slash
        with tarfile.open(tar_path, mode='w:gz') as tar:
            dirinfo = tarfile.TarInfo(name="mydir/")
            dirinfo.type = tarfile.DIRTYPE
            dirinfo.mode = 0o755
            tar.addfile(tarinfo=dirinfo)

        # Verify the exact behavior
        with tarfile.open(tar_path, mode='r') as tar:
            # Exact match works
            member = tar.getmember("mydir/")
            assert member.name == "mydir/"

            # Without trailing slash fails
            with pytest.raises(KeyError):
                tar.getmember("mydir")

        # Now test the opposite: tar with no trailing slash
        tar_path2 = os.path.join(tmpdir, "test2.tar.gz")
        with tarfile.open(tar_path2, mode='w:gz') as tar:
            dirinfo = tarfile.TarInfo(name="mydir")
            dirinfo.type = tarfile.DIRTYPE
            dirinfo.mode = 0o755
            tar.addfile(tarinfo=dirinfo)

        with tarfile.open(tar_path2, mode='r') as tar:
            # Without trailing slash works
            member = tar.getmember("mydir")
            assert member.name == "mydir"

            # With trailing slash fails
            with pytest.raises(KeyError):
                tar.getmember("mydir/")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    pytest.main([__file__, "-v"])

"""
Regression test for Galaxy collection tar directory extraction fragility.

This test demonstrates the issue where collection extraction fails when
tar archive directory entries have trailing path separators that don't
match what the code expects.

Issue: The _extract_tar_dir function in lib/ansible/galaxy/collection/__init__.py
calls tar.getmember(dirname) directly without normalizing trailing slashes.
This creates a fragile situation where:

1. FILES.json might list directories with trailing slashes (e.g., "plugins/")
2. But the tar archive stores the directory name in a different format
3. When getmember() is called, the lookup fails with KeyError

The root cause is that the code uses a direct getmember() call without
any robust lookup strategy that accounts for naming variations.
"""
import json
import os
import tarfile
import tempfile
from pathlib import Path

import pytest

def test_extract_tar_dir_with_trailing_slash_mismatch():
    """
    Test that extraction fails when directory names in FILES.json have trailing
    slashes but the tar archive stores them without trailing slashes.

    This demonstrates the fragility of the _extract_tar_dir implementation which
    calls tar.getmember(dirname) directly without normalizing trailing slashes.

    The issue: When FILES.json lists "plugins/" (with trailing slash)
    but tar has "plugins" (without), getmember() will raise KeyError.
    """
    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:
        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, "test_collection.tar.gz")

        # Create a tar file without trailing slashes in directory names
        create_collection_tar_without_dir_trailing_slash(tar_path)

        # Try to extract using tar.getmember() with mismatched names
        with tarfile.open(tar_path, mode='r') as tar:
            # The archive stores it as "test-collection-1.0.0/plugins" (no trailing slash)
            # But FILES.json might list it as "plugins/" (with trailing slash)
            # The collection root prefix from tar is "test-collection-1.0.0"
            dirname_with_slash = "test-collection-1.0.0/plugins/"
            dirname_without_slash = "test-collection-1.0.0/plugins"

            # This fails with KeyError because tar.getmember requires exact match
            # and the tar has the name without trailing slash
            with pytest.raises(KeyError):
                tar.getmember(dirname_with_slash)

            # This succeeds because the tar has it without slash
            member = tar.getmember(dirname_without_slash)
            assert member.name == dirname_without_slash
            assert member.isdir()

def test_tarfile_getmember_fragile_to_trailing_slashes():
    """
    Test that demonstrates the fragility of tar.getmember() with trailing slashes.

    When looking up a directory in a tar file, the member name must match exactly.
    If the tar stores "plugins" but code looks for "plugins/", it will raise KeyError.

    This is the fundamental issue that makes collection extraction fragile.
    """
    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:
        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, "test.tar.gz")

        # Create a tar with a directory entry (without trailing slash)
        with tarfile.open(tar_path, mode='w:gz') as tar:
            dirinfo = tarfile.TarInfo(name="test-1.0.0/plugins")
            dirinfo.type = tarfile.DIRTYPE
            dirinfo.mode = 0o755
            tar.addfile(tarinfo=dirinfo)

        # Verify the fragility: getmember requires exact match
        with tarfile.open(tar_path, mode='r') as tar:
            # These work - exact match
            member = tar.getmember("test-1.0.0/plugins")
            assert member.isdir()

            # This fails - no exact match
            with pytest.raises(KeyError):
                # If code tries to look for "plugins/" (with trailing slash)
                # when tar has "plugins" (without), it fails
                tar.getmember("test-1.0.0/plugins/")


def test_extract_tar_dir_with_trailing_slash_mismatch():
    """
    Test that extraction fails when directory names in FILES.json have trailing
    slashes but the tar archive stores them without trailing slashes.

    This demonstrates the fragility of the _extract_tar_dir implementation which
    calls tar.getmember(dirname) directly without normalizing trailing slashes.

    Scenario: FILES.json lists "plugins/" (with trailing slash) but tar has "plugins" (without)
    Result: getmember() will raise KeyError because it requires an exact match
    """
    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:
        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, "test_collection.tar.gz")

        # Create a tar file without trailing slashes in directory names
        create_collection_tar_without_dir_trailing_slash(tar_path)

        # Simulate what happens in install_artifact when FILES.json has "plugins/"
        with tarfile.open(tar_path, mode='r') as tar:
            # FILES.json lists directory with trailing slash
            dirname_from_files_json = "test-collection-1.0.0/plugins/"

            # But tar has it without trailing slash (tarfile normalizes)
            # So direct getmember() call fails
            with pytest.raises(KeyError):
                tar.getmember(dirname_from_files_json)

            # This succeeds (exact match)
            member = tar.getmember("test-collection-1.0.0/plugins")
            assert member.isdir()

Regression test for Galaxy collection tar directory extraction fragility.

This test demonstrates the issue where collection extraction fails when
tar archive directory entries have trailing path separators that don't
match what the code expects.

Issue: The _extract_tar_dir function in lib/ansible/galaxy/collection/__init__.py
calls tar.getmember(dirname) directly without normalizing trailing slashes.
This creates a fragile situation where:

1. FILES.json might list directories with trailing slashes (e.g., "plugins/")
2. But the tar archive stores the directory name in a different format
3. When getmember() is called, the lookup fails with KeyError

The root cause is that the code uses a direct getmember() call without
any robust lookup strategy that accounts for naming variations.
"""
import json
import os
import tarfile
import tempfile
from pathlib import Path

import pytest


def test_extract_tar_dir_with_trailing_slash_mismatch():
    """
    Test that extraction fails when directory names in FILES.json have trailing
    slashes but the tar archive stores them without trailing slashes.

    This demonstrates the fragility of the _extract_tar_dir implementation which
    calls tar.getmember(dirname) directly without normalizing trailing slashes.

    The issue: When FILES.json lists "plugins/" (with trailing slash)
    but tar has "plugins" (without), getmember() will raise KeyError.
    """
    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:
        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, "test_collection.tar.gz")

        # Create a tar file without trailing slashes in directory names
        create_collection_tar_without_dir_trailing_slash(tar_path)

        # Try to extract using tar.getmember() with mismatched names
        with tarfile.open(tar_path, mode='r') as tar:
            # The archive stores it as "test-collection-1.0.0/plugins" (no trailing slash)
            # But FILES.json might list it as "plugins/" (with trailing slash)
            # The collection root prefix from tar is "test-collection-1.0.0"
            dirname_with_slash = "test-collection-1.0.0/plugins/"
            dirname_without_slash = "test-collection-1.0.0/plugins"

            # This fails with KeyError because tar.getmember requires exact match
            # and the tar has the name without trailing slash
            with pytest.raises(KeyError):
                tar.getmember(dirname_with_slash)

            # This succeeds because the tar has it without slash
            member = tar.getmember(dirname_without_slash)
            assert member.name == dirname_without_slash
            assert member.isdir()


def test_extract_tar_dir_with_trailing_slash_in_archive():
    """
    Test that extraction might fail when directory names in tar archive have
    trailing slashes but the code tries to look them up without slashes.

    This demonstrates the opposite scenario of the fragility.
    """
    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:
        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, "test_collection.tar.gz")
        extract_dir = os.path.join(tmpdir, "extracted")
        os.makedirs(extract_dir)

        # Create a tar file with trailing slashes in directory names
        create_collection_tar_with_dir_trailing_slash(tar_path)

        # Try to extract using tar.getmember with different forms
        with tarfile.open(tar_path, mode='r') as tar:
            # The tar has "test-collection-1.0.0/plugins/"
            dirname_with_slash = "test-collection-1.0.0/plugins/"
            dirname_without_slash = "test-collection-1.0.0/plugins"

            # This succeeds because the tar has it with slash
            member = tar.getmember(dirname_with_slash)
            assert member.name == dirname_with_slash
            assert member.isdir()

            # This will fail because tar has the name WITH slash
            # but we're looking for it without slash
            with pytest.raises(KeyError):
                tar.getmember(dirname_without_slash)


def test_tar_getmember_requires_exact_match():
    """
    Test demonstrating that tarfile.getmember() requires an exact match
    of the member name, including trailing slashes.

    This is the root cause of the fragility - directories can be stored
    with or without trailing slashes, and the lookup will fail if there's
    a mismatch.
    """
    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:
        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, "test.tar.gz")

        # Create a simple tar with a directory that has a trailing slash
        with tarfile.open(tar_path, mode='w:gz') as tar:
            dirinfo = tarfile.TarInfo(name="mydir/")
            dirinfo.type = tarfile.DIRTYPE
            dirinfo.mode = 0o755
            tar.addfile(tarinfo=dirinfo)

        # Verify the exact behavior
        with tarfile.open(tar_path, mode='r') as tar:
            # Exact match works
            member = tar.getmember("mydir/")
            assert member.name == "mydir/"

            # Without trailing slash fails
            with pytest.raises(KeyError):
                tar.getmember("mydir")

        # Now test the opposite: tar with no trailing slash
        tar_path2 = os.path.join(tmpdir, "test2.tar.gz")
        with tarfile.open(tar_path2, mode='w:gz') as tar:
            dirinfo = tarfile.TarInfo(name="mydir")
            dirinfo.type = tarfile.DIRTYPE
            dirinfo.mode = 0o755
            tar.addfile(tarinfo=dirinfo)

        with tarfile.open(tar_path2, mode='r') as tar:
            # Without trailing slash works
            member = tar.getmember("mydir")
            assert member.name == "mydir"

            # With trailing slash fails
            with pytest.raises(KeyError):
                tar.getmember("mydir/")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    pytest.main([__file__, "-v"])

1. FILES.json might list directories with trailing slashes (e.g., "plugins/")
2. But the tar archive stores the directory name in a different format
3. When getmember() is called, the lookup fails with KeyError

The root cause is that the code uses a direct getmember() call without
any robust lookup strategy that accounts for naming variations.
"""
import json
import os
import tarfile
import tempfile
from pathlib import Path

import pytest


def test_extract_tar_dir_with_trailing_slash_mismatch():
    """
    Test that extraction fails when directory names in FILES.json have trailing
    slashes but the tar archive stores them without trailing slashes.

    This demonstrates the fragility of the _extract_tar_dir implementation which
    calls tar.getmember(dirname) directly without normalizing trailing slashes.

    The issue: When FILES.json lists "plugins/" (with trailing slash)
    but tar has "plugins" (without), getmember() will raise KeyError.
    """
    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:
        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, "test_collection.tar.gz")

        # Create a tar file without trailing slashes in directory names
        create_collection_tar_without_dir_trailing_slash(tar_path)

        # Try to extract using tar.getmember() with mismatched names
        with tarfile.open(tar_path, mode='r') as tar:
            # The archive stores it as "test-collection-1.0.0/plugins" (no trailing slash)
            # But FILES.json might list it as "plugins/" (with trailing slash)
            # The collection root prefix from tar is "test-collection-1.0.0"
            dirname_with_slash = "test-collection-1.0.0/plugins/"
            dirname_without_slash = "test-collection-1.0.0/plugins"

            # This fails with KeyError because tar.getmember requires exact match
            # and the tar has the name without trailing slash
            with pytest.raises(KeyError):
                tar.getmember(dirname_with_slash)

            # This succeeds because the tar has it without slash
            member = tar.getmember(dirname_without_slash)
            assert member.name == dirname_without_slash
            assert member.isdir()


def test_extract_tar_dir_with_trailing_slash_in_archive():
    """
    Test that extraction might fail when directory names in tar archive have
    trailing slashes but the code tries to look them up without slashes.

    This demonstrates the opposite scenario of the fragility.
    """
    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:
        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, "test_collection.tar.gz")
        extract_dir = os.path.join(tmpdir, "extracted")
        os.makedirs(extract_dir)

        # Create a tar file with trailing slashes in directory names
        create_collection_tar_with_dir_trailing_slash(tar_path)

        # Try to extract using tar.getmember with different forms
        with tarfile.open(tar_path, mode='r') as tar:
            # The tar has "test-collection-1.0.0/plugins/"
            dirname_with_slash = "test-collection-1.0.0/plugins/"
            dirname_without_slash = "test-collection-1.0.0/plugins"

            # This succeeds because the tar has it with slash
            member = tar.getmember(dirname_with_slash)
            assert member.name == dirname_with_slash
            assert member.isdir()

            # This will fail because tar has the name WITH slash
            # but we're looking for it without slash
            with pytest.raises(KeyError):
                tar.getmember(dirname_without_slash)


def test_tar_getmember_requires_exact_match():
    """
    Test demonstrating that tarfile.getmember() requires an exact match
    of the member name, including trailing slashes.

    This is the root cause of the fragility - directories can be stored
    with or without trailing slashes, and the lookup will fail if there's
    a mismatch.
    """
    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:
        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, "test.tar.gz")

        # Create a simple tar with a directory that has a trailing slash
        with tarfile.open(tar_path, mode='w:gz') as tar:
            dirinfo = tarfile.TarInfo(name="mydir/")
            dirinfo.type = tarfile.DIRTYPE
            dirinfo.mode = 0o755
            tar.addfile(tarinfo=dirinfo)

        # Verify the exact behavior
        with tarfile.open(tar_path, mode='r') as tar:
            # Exact match works
            member = tar.getmember("mydir/")
            assert member.name == "mydir/"

            # Without trailing slash fails
            with pytest.raises(KeyError):
                tar.getmember("mydir")

        # Now test the opposite: tar with no trailing slash
        tar_path2 = os.path.join(tmpdir, "test2.tar.gz")
        with tarfile.open(tar_path2, mode='w:gz') as tar:
            dirinfo = tarfile.TarInfo(name="mydir")
            dirinfo.type = tarfile.DIRTYPE
            dirinfo.mode = 0o755
            tar.addfile(tarinfo=dirinfo)

        with tarfile.open(tar_path2, mode='r') as tar:
            # Without trailing slash works
            member = tar.getmember("mydir")
            assert member.name == "mydir"

            # With trailing slash fails
            with pytest.raises(KeyError):
                tar.getmember("mydir/")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    pytest.main([__file__, "-v"])

"""
Regression test for Galaxy collection tar directory extraction fragility.

This test demonstrates the issue where collection extraction fails when
tar archive directory entries have trailing path separators that don't
match what the code expects.

Issue: The _extract_tar_dir function in lib/ansible/galaxy/collection/__init__.py
calls tar.getmember(dirname) directly without normalizing trailing slashes.
This creates a fragile situation where:

1. FILES.json might list directories with trailing slashes (e.g., "plugins/")
2. But the tar archive stores the directory name in a different format
3. When getmember() is called, the lookup fails with KeyError

any robust lookup strategy that accounts for naming variations.

    calls tar.getmember(dirname) directly without normalizing trailing slashes.

    but tar has "plugins" (without), getmember() will raise KeyError.

            dirname_without_slash = "test-collection-1.0.0/plugins"

def test_collection_tar_extraction_with_directory_mismatch():
    """
    Test demonstrating that collection installation fails when directory entries
    in FILES.json don't match the actual tar member names due to trailing slash differences.

    This directly shows the problem: _extract_tar_dir() calls tar.getmember(dirname)
    which fails if dirname includes a trailing slash but tar doesn't have it (or vice versa).
    """
    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:
        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, "test_collection.tar.gz")

        # Create a tar file without trailing slashes in directory names
        # (This is what tarfile.add() does - it normalizes directory names)
        create_collection_tar_without_dir_trailing_slash(tar_path)

        # Simulate the collection installation process
        with tarfile.open(tar_path, mode='r') as tar:
            # In install_artifact(), it iterates files from FILES.json
            # FILES.json might have directories with trailing slashes
            files_from_json = [
                {"name": "test-collection-1.0.0/plugins/", "ftype": "dir"},
                {"name": "test-collection-1.0.0/plugins/modules/", "ftype": "dir"},
            ]

            for file_info in files_from_json:

Regression test for Galaxy collection tar directory extraction fragility.

This test demonstrates the issue where collection extraction fails when
tar archive directory entries have trailing path separators that don't
match what the code expects.

Issue: The _extract_tar_dir function in lib/ansible/galaxy/collection/__init__.py
calls tar.getmember(dirname) directly without normalizing trailing slashes.
This creates a fragile situation where:

1. FILES.json might list directories with trailing slashes (e.g., "plugins/")
2. But the tar archive stores the directory name in a different format
3. When getmember() is called, the lookup fails with KeyError

The root cause is that the code uses a direct getmember() call without
any robust lookup strategy that accounts for naming variations.
"""
import json
import os
import tarfile
import tempfile
from pathlib import Path

import pytest


def test_tarfile_getmember_fragile_to_trailing_slashes():
    """
    Test that demonstrates the fragility of tar.getmember() with trailing slashes.

    When looking up a directory in a tar file, the member name must match exactly.
    If the tar stores "plugins" but code looks for "plugins/", it will raise KeyError.

    This is the fundamental issue that makes collection extraction fragile.
    """
    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:
        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, "test.tar.gz")

        # Create a tar with a directory entry (without trailing slash)
        with tarfile.open(tar_path, mode='w:gz') as tar:
            dirinfo = tarfile.TarInfo(name="test-1.0.0/plugins")
            dirinfo.type = tarfile.DIRTYPE
            dirinfo.mode = 0o755
            tar.addfile(tarinfo=dirinfo)

        # Verify the fragility: getmember requires exact match
        with tarfile.open(tar_path, mode='r') as tar:
            # These work - exact match
            member = tar.getmember("test-1.0.0/plugins")
            assert member.isdir()

            # This fails - no exact match
            with pytest.raises(KeyError):
                # If code tries to look for "plugins/" (with trailing slash)
                # when tar has "plugins" (without), it fails
                tar.getmember("test-1.0.0/plugins/")


def test_extract_tar_dir_with_trailing_slash_mismatch():
    """
    Test that extraction fails when directory names in FILES.json have trailing
    slashes but the tar archive stores them without trailing slashes.

    This demonstrates the fragility of the _extract_tar_dir implementation which
    calls tar.getmember(dirname) directly without normalizing trailing slashes.

    Scenario: FILES.json lists "plugins/" (with trailing slash) but tar has "plugins" (without)
    Result: getmember() will raise KeyError because it requires an exact match
    """
    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:
        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, "test_collection.tar.gz")

        # Create a tar file without trailing slashes in directory names
        create_collection_tar_without_dir_trailing_slash(tar_path)

        # Simulate what happens in install_artifact when FILES.json has "plugins/"
        with tarfile.open(tar_path, mode='r') as tar:
            # FILES.json lists directory with trailing slash
            dirname_from_files_json = "test-collection-1.0.0/plugins/"

            # But tar has it without trailing slash (tarfile normalizes)
            # So direct getmember() call fails
            with pytest.raises(KeyError):
                tar.getmember(dirname_from_files_json)

            # This succeeds (exact match)
            member = tar.getmember("test-collection-1.0.0/plugins")
            assert member.isdir()
